reasons: Esmeralda is a strong answer because Hanabi's safety depends heavily on having shield active.
Removing or pressuring shield value also weakens Hanabi's crowd-control immunity window.

proof.summary: Hanabi gains control immunity while shielded; Esmeralda can pressure shield-based safety and make Hanabi easier to control or burst.
worksBestWhen: Esmeralda reaches Hanabi before Hanabi free-hits in teamfight.
Allies follow up once Hanabi's shield window is weakened.
failureCases: Hanabi stays far behind strong peel.
Esmeralda dives alone into full enemy formation.

reasons: Natalia can punish Hanabi before Hanabi gets a safe teamfight position.
Hanabi is vulnerable if caught without shield or before she can lifesteal through the fight.

proof.summary: Hanabi wants protected backline uptime; Natalia can threaten her from flank vision and force defensive positioning before the fight starts.
worksBestWhen: Natalia attacks when Hanabi is separated from tank or support.
failureCases: Hanabi keeps shield active and stays near peel.
Natalia reveals too early and loses flank pressure.

reasons: Lolita can reduce Hanabi's ability to freely damage the backline with ranged attacks.
She also helps force Hanabi to reposition instead of standing still and bouncing damage through grouped allies.

proof.summary: Hanabi wants grouped enemies and repeated ranged uptime; Lolita can protect the team's approach and deny clean firing angles.
worksBestWhen: Lolita faces Hanabi during objective standoffs.
Teammates avoid stacking too closely behind minion waves.
failureCases: Hanabi attacks from a side angle.
Lolita uses shield before Hanabi commits damage.

reasons: Belerick can punish repeated basic-attack uptime and stand in Hanabi's firing path.
His frontline presence makes it harder for Hanabi to safely hit priority targets.

proof.summary: Hanabi wants repeated backline basic attacks; Belerick can absorb pressure and force Hanabi to spend damage on a durable frontline instead.
worksBestWhen: Belerick stays between Hanabi and the squishy target.
The team avoids clumping too much for Hanabi's bounce damage.
failureCases: Hanabi free-farms items and stays outside Belerick's engage range.

reasons: Pharsa can outrange Hanabi and punish her immobile teamfight positioning.
Hanabi wants enemies grouped; Pharsa can force Hanabi's team to split or retreat.

proof.summary: Hanabi wants protected basic-attack uptime, while Pharsa can pressure from artillery range before Hanabi can hit back.
worksBestWhen: Pharsa casts from outside Hanabi's attack range.
The team protects Pharsa from flank divers.
failureCases: Hanabi's team dives Pharsa before she casts freely.
Pharsa misses ultimate hits and Hanabi keeps free-hitting.
